text.*:
Home * Engine Testing * Test-Positions

Note: In recent years testing positions has fallen out of favor among top engine developers. SPRT tests are now generally regarded as a superior method for engine testing.

There are several classical and new developed test-position suites available as

  • Forsyth-Edwards Notation (FEN)
  • Extended Position Description (EPD)
  • Portable Game Notation (PGN)

1950 ...[edit]

  • Fred Reinfeld (1958, 2001). Win at Chess. Dover Books on Chess » Win at Chess

1970 ...[edit]

  • F. Donald Bloss (1973). Rate your own Chess. Van Nostrand Reinhold Inc.
  • Gary L. Ratliff (1979). Practical Rating Program. Personal Computing, Vol. 3, No. 9, pp. 62 » Bloss
  • Frieder Schwenkel (1979). Berating the ratings system. Personal Computing, Vol. 3, No. 11, pp. 77 » Ratliff, Bloss
  • John Shaposka (1979). "JS" Takes the Bloss Test. Personal Computing, Vol. 3, No. 12, pp. 75 » Ratliff, Bloss
  • Editor (1979). Chess Ratings from Problem Solving. Personal Computing, Vol. 3, No. 12, pp. 77 » CAPS II, Chess Problems, Compositions and Studies

1980 ...[edit]

  • Floyd R. Kirk (1980). Bloss Flunks Test. Personal Computing, Vol. 4, No. 8, pp. 72 » Ratliff, Bloss
  • W. W. Foster (1980). Six Test Problems for Chess Playing Machines. Personal Computing, Vol. 4, No. 10, pp. 81
  • Ivan Bratko, Danny Kopec (1982). A Test for Comparison of Human and Computer Performance in Chess. Advances in Computer Chess 3

1990 ...[edit]

  • Tony Marsland (1990). The Bratko-Kopec Test Revisited. ICCA Journal, Vol. 13, No. 1
  • Kevin J. Lang, Warren D. Smith (1993). A Test Suite for Chess Programs. ps, ChessTest2.tar.Z [14]
  • Shawn Benn, Danny Kopec (1993). The Bratko-Kopec Test Recalibrated. ICCA Journal, Vol. 16, No. 3, pdf
  • Larry Kaufman (1994). The One-Hour CCR Test. Computer Chess Reports, Vol. 4, No. 2, pp. 25-30
  • Kevin J. Lang, Warren D. Smith (1995). A Test Suite for Chess Programs. ICCA Journal, Vol. 16, No. 3
